content.title: 下午 (xiàwǔ) — afternoon
content.body:
**下午** (xiàwǔ) means "afternoon" and refers to the time from after lunch (around 1 PM) until about 6 PM.

Usage:
- 我**下午**上课。(I have class in the afternoon.)
- **下午**三点 (3 o'clock in the afternoon/3 PM)

The character 下 means "down/below/after" and 午 means "noon," indicating the time after noon.

content.title: 再 (zài) — then/after that (in sequence)
content.body:
**再** (zài) in this context means "then" or "after that" when describing a sequence of actions. It appears before the second verb.

Usage:
- 我先吃饭，**再**看书。(I first eat, then read.)
- 他先工作，**再**休息。(He first works, then rests.)

Note: 再 has other meanings in different contexts, but in 先...再... patterns

content.title: Planning a weekend day
content.body:
When planning activities for a weekend, you use the same patterns but might have more varied activities:

**明天是周末。我早上先睡懒觉，然后起床。上午我去咖啡店。中午我和朋友吃饭。下午我先看电影，再去书店。晚上我在家看书。**

(Tomorrow is the weekend. In the morning I first sleep in, then get up. In the late morning I go to a coffee shop. At noon I eat wi

content.title: Time words come before verbs in Chinese
content.body:
In Chinese, time expressions appear **before** the verb, not at the end of the sentence like in English. The pattern is: **Subject + Time + Verb + Object**.

Examples:
- 我**明天**去。(I go tomorrow.) — NOT 我去明天
- 他**今天**工作。(He works today.)
- 我们**现在**吃。(We eat now.)

Time words can also appear at the ve

content.title: Combining time of day with sequencing
content.body:
You can combine specific times of day with sequence words to create detailed descriptions of your routine.

Pattern options:
- **Time + Subject + 先...再...**
- **Subject + Time + 先...再...**

Examples:
- **早上**我**先**喝咖啡，**再**吃早饭。(In the morning, I first drink coffee, then eat breakfast.)
- 我**晚上先**做作业

content.title: 早上 (zǎoshang) — early morning
content.body:
**早上** (zǎoshang) means "early morning" and refers to the time from dawn until about 8-9 AM.

Usage:
- 我**早上**喝咖啡。(I drink coffee in the morning.)
- **早上**好！(Good morning!)

The character 早 means "early" and 上 means "up/above," together forming "early morning."

content.title: 先 (xiān) — first/before
content.body:
**先** (xiān) means "first" or "before" and is used to indicate the first action in a sequence. It appears before the verb it modifies.

Usage:
- 我**先**吃饭。(I eat first.)
- 他**先**工作。(He works first.)

先 is often paired with 再 (then/after that) to show a clear sequence of two actions.

content.title: A typical weekday routine
content.body:
Here's how to describe a complete daily routine using all the time expressions and sequencing patterns:

**我每天早上七点起床。我先喝水，再吃早饭，然后去工作。我上午九点开始工作。中午十二点我吃午饭。下午我工作。晚上六点我回家。我晚上先做晚饭，再吃饭，然后看电视。我十点睡觉。**

(Every day I get up at 7 AM. I first drink water, then eat breakfast, then go to work. I start work at 9

content.title: 然后 (ránhòu) — then/after that
content.body:
**然后** (ránhòu) means "then" or "after that" and connects sequential actions or events. It's similar to 再 but slightly more formal and often used to connect longer sequences or clauses.

Usage:
- 我吃饭，**然后**看书。(I eat, then read.)
- 他工作，**然后**回家。(He works, then goes home.)

然后 can appear at the beginn

content.title: 中午 (zhōngwǔ) — noon/midday
content.body:
**中午** (zhōngwǔ) means "noon" or "midday," typically referring to the time around 12 PM and the lunch period.

Usage:
- 我**中午**吃饭。(I eat at noon/lunchtime.)
- **中午**十二点 (12 o'clock noon)

The character 中 means "middle/center" and 午 means "noon," clearly indicating the middle of the day.

content.title: 每天 (měitiān) — every day
content.body:
**每天** (měitiān) means "every day" or "daily." It follows the same word order rules as other time expressions.

Usage:
- 我**每天**学习。(I study every day.)
- 他**每天**工作。(He works every day.)

The character 每 means "every/each" and 天 means "day."

content.title: Describing a daily schedule
content.body:
When describing a daily schedule in Chinese, combine time-of-day words with verbs to create a natural flow. Remember that time expressions come before verbs.

Pattern: **Subject + Time + Verb + Object**

Example daily routine:
- 我**早上**七点起床。(I get up at 7 AM.)
- 我**上午**九点工作。(I work at 9 AM.)
- 我**中午

content.title: 晚上 (wǎnshang) — evening/night
content.body:
**晚上** (wǎnshang) means "evening" or "night" and refers to the time from about 6 PM until bedtime or midnight.

Usage:
- 我**晚上**看书。(I read in the evening.)
- **晚上**好！(Good evening!)
- 我**晚上**十点睡觉。(I go to bed at 10 PM.)

The character 晚 means "late/evening" and 上 suggests the onset or beginning, tog

content.title: Common errors with time expressions
content.body:
Common mistakes learners make with time expressions:

❌ **Wrong:** 我去学校明天。(Time at the end)
✓ **Correct:** 我明天去学校。or 明天我去学校。

❌ **Wrong:** 我先吃饭和再看书。(Using 和 "and" instead of sequence markers)
✓ **Correct:** 我先吃饭，再看书。

❌ **Wrong:** 我再吃饭，先看书。(Reversed sequence)
✓ **Correct:** 我先看书，再吃饭。(I first read, t

content.title: Sequencing multiple actions with 先...再... and 然后
content.body:
To describe a sequence of three or more actions, combine 先...再... with 然后 or use multiple 然后 connectors.

Patterns:
- **先 + Verb₁, 再 + Verb₂, 然后 + Verb₃**
- **先 + Verb₁, 然后 + Verb₂, 然后 + Verb₃**

Examples:
- 我**先**吃饭，**再**喝茶，**然后**看书。(I first eat, then drink tea, then read.)
- 他**先**起床，**然后**喝咖啡，**然

content.title: Asking and answering about daily schedules
content.body:
In conversations, you'll need to ask and answer questions about schedules:

**Questions:**
- 你每天几点起床？(What time do you get up every day?)
- 你早上做什么？(What do you do in the morning?)
- 你先吃饭还是先工作？(Do you eat first or work first?) [Note: Verify that 还是 (or) is within the subtopic scope before using this

content.title: 上午 (shàngwǔ) — late morning/forenoon
content.body:
**上午** (shàngwǔ) refers to the late morning period, roughly from 9 AM to noon. It's the second part of the morning, after 早上.

Usage:
- 我**上午**工作。(I work in the late morning/forenoon.)
- **上午**十点 (10 o'clock in the morning)

The character 上 means "up/first" and 午 means "noon," together indicating th
